stories.summary: Michelle Gisin describes her severe injuries from the December 11, 2025 accident at Corviglia: unstable cervical spine fracture, knee injuries with ACL and meniscus tears, hand injuries with three dislocated carpal bones, and shoulder damage with torn tendons. She explains the extent of injuries and notes that fortunately healing has progressed well independently.

stories.short_label: Transfer to Zurich and expert medical team
stories.summary: Gisin explains the decision to transfer to Zurich for treatment and her fortunate connection with Prof. Keel and specialists Prof. Schweizer and Dr. Hahn. She discusses the complexity of her hand injuries, the attempts to determine thumb mobility, and the reassurance provided by medical professionals before surgery. Prof. Keel gave her confidence that everything would be okay.

Besuch der Klasse 6B aus Schafisheim im SBB-Schulzug zur Sensibilisierung über Stromgefahren. Schüler erleben Demonstration der Gefahren von Starkstrom. Schüleraussagen zur Warnung vor Trainsurfing und Unverständnis für diesen Trend.

SVP party held delegates assembly in Maienfeld, Graubünden to promote initiative against 10-million-person Switzerland coming to ballot in June. SVP protests loss of farmland and housing shortage, claiming Switzerland is being over-concreted like Singapore. Initiative would require federal government to take measures once population reaches 9.5 million, including potential termination of freedom of movement agreement with EU. Delegates voted unanimously without abstentions to support the initiative.

Yemeni Houthi rebels claim first military operation against Israel, firing barrage of ballistic missiles at sensitive military targets. Israel claims to have intercepted the projectiles. This marks significant escalation as Houthis, allied with Iran, enter the ongoing US-Israel-Iran war. While Iran controls Strait of Hormuz, concerns grow that Houthi allies could disrupt Red Sea maritime chokepoint Strait of Bab al-Mandab, though their military capability for this remains questionable.

stories.short_label: Die Mitte's Marriage Tax Initiative Full Story
stories.summary: Die Mitte party held delegates assembly in Zug and decided to continue with their 'Fairness Initiative' (Für faire Bundessteuern) for federal taxes. Initiative would allow married couples to combine income without tax penalty compared to unmarried couples. This contradicts the individual taxation approved by voters three weeks ago. Party president Philipp Matthias Bregy argues their model is fairer, more modern, and easier to implement. Voting expected in autumn. FDP co-president expresses concern about potential confusion between cantonal and federal taxation systems.

Millions of Americans nationwide participate in 'No-Kings' protests against President Donald Trump's policies on third such protest day. Tens of thousands demonstrate in Washington DC with over 3,000 additional protests planned across the country. Protesters demand democracy, peace, oppose ICE immigration enforcement and war. This is the third major mobilization since last summer with hopes to influence midterm elections. Organizers claim it will be largest non-violent protest day in US history. Trump administration dismisses protests, but mass mobilization indicates genuine popular discontent. USA Correspondent Roger Aebli analyzes that despite impressive demonstrations, mass protests during Trump's first term did not prevent his reelection. Trump's Iran war provides new ammunition for Democrats, but success in autumn elections is not guaranteed.

Documented filmmaker Pavel Talankin awarded Oscar two weeks ago for 'Mr Nobody Against Putin' but was designated foreign agent by Russian Justice Ministry yesterday. Russian regime uses foreign agent label to discredit NGOs and silence critics. 83-year-old human rights activist Swetlana Gannuschkina, who leads Civic Assistance organization providing help to refugees and migrants in Moscow, was also personally labeled foreign agent. Gannuschkina is sharp critic of Russian invasion of Ukraine. Organization receives foreign funding making it subject to foreign agent designation. Label brings multiple difficulties: organization financing severely hampered, operational field constrained by legal, administrative, and financial requirements. Organization provides legal support, humanitarian aid, and integration courses for refugees and migrants. Gannuschkina maintains their work is not foreign agency but service to people living in Russia, with clear state benefits. UN has drastically cut financial support to organization due to Trump administration budget cuts, further constraining NGO operations.

Documentary film 'Unbekannter Bekannter' (Unknown Acquaintance) about Kurt Hirschfeld, Jewish theater director and later director of Zurich Schauspielhaus during Nazi era. Hirschfeld created theater with political resistance stance during WWII while facing pressure to not appear too political. Theater became refuge where he hired many actors fleeing Nazi persecution, saving their lives. Despite founding Kurt-Hirschfeld-Weg street behind Oerlikon train station, Hirschfeld is largely forgotten. Directors Samir and Stina Werenfels created documentary to bring him back to public consciousness. Film includes testimony from his daughter Ruth Hirschfeld recalling the day he became director with Max Frisch present. Hirschfeld remained in background as number 2, possibly contributing to historical obscurity. Documentary aims to restore his legacy as cultural resistor during dark historical period.

Erklärung von speziellen Wolkenformationen (Föhnfische und UFO-ähnliche Linsenwolken), die durch Südföhn und Windströmungen über die Alpen entstehen. Nutzer haben Fotos aus dem Wallis und anderen Alpenregionen eingereicht.
Kaltfront zieht in die Schweiz. In der Nacht Regen und Schneefall mit Schneefallgrenze bei etwa 400 m. Rutschgefahr im Flachland möglich. Im Mittelland wenig Schnee zu erwarten.

stories.short_label: Montag: Wechsel aus Sonne und Wolken
stories.summary: Montag startet teils bewölkt in den Bergen mit Schneeschauern, tagsüber trockener mit sonnigen Abschnitten. Graubünden länger bewölkt. Westschweiz und Süden sonniger mit Nordföhn und etwa 15°C im Rhonetal. Norden bleibt kühl mit Bise über 70 km/h.

stories.short_label: Neue Woche: Unbeständig mit langsamer Erwärmung
stories.summary: Neue Woche bringt unbeständiges Wetter mit wiederholten Störungen und Regen- sowie Schneeschauern. Wind dreht auf Nordwest. Dienstag und Mittwoch bleiben unbeständig. Temperaturen steigen allmählich. Süden bleibt häufig sonnig.
Am Osterwochenende wird es voraussichtlich größtenteils trocken sein. Die Aussichten sind allerdings noch mit Unsicherheiten behaftet.

Der Gemeindevorstand von Zuoz empfiehlt, nicht in die neue Gesundheitsversorgung für die Region einzutreten, da die Gemeinde bei der Ausarbeitung nicht einbezogen wurde und zu wenig Informationen erhielt. Die restlichen zehn Gemeinden des Oberengadins haben sich bereits dafür ausgesprochen.

In Thusis hat eine 29-jährige Frau eine 87-jährige Seniorin mit einem Messer schwer verletzt. Augenzeugen hielten die Angreiferin fest bis zum Eintreffen der Polizei. Die verletzte Frau wurde ins Kantonsspital Chur gebracht. Die beiden Frauen standen in keiner Beziehung zueinander.

Der Fernsehsender TeleBielingue verliert seine Konzession für das Versorgungsgebiet Biel und Umgebung zugunsten des neuen Senders Canal B. Das Bundesverwaltungsgericht weist die Beschwerde von TeleBielingue ab. Canal B geht ab Juli auf Sendung.
